response: This code implements iterative binary search, a divide-and-conquer algorithm that requires a pre-sorted input array to locate a target value in O(log n) time, far faster than a linear search for large datasets. It repeatedly narrows its search range by comparing the middle element of the current low-to-high bounds to the target, adjusting the bounds to eliminate half the unneeded search space each loop, returning the target's index if found or -1 if the target does not exist in the array.
